title: Exit Through The Kwik E Mart

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening sequence catchphrase moment
jokes.punchline: Homer's iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Iconic Simpsons catchphrase - high character integration and quotability but not original to this episode

jokes.text: Yes! Awake! Let's do this!
jokes.setup: Lisa waking up Homer who was presumably sleeping
jokes.punchline: Homer's overly enthusiastic response to being woken up
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer enthusiasm misplaced - functional but not remarkable

jokes.text: It's time to start your annual mad dash around town to get her a present that's not completely insulting
jokes.setup: Lisa warning Homer about Marge's birthday
jokes.punchline: The specific qualifier 'not completely insulting' reveals Homer's track record
jokes.explanation: Sharp character observation with good specificity - 'not completely insulting' is a perfect qualifier

jokes.text: I've already bought your mother a present. And brace yourself, I put some thought into it.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to be prepared and thoughtful
jokes.punchline: The need to tell Lisa to 'brace yourself' for the shocking news that he put thought into it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's self-awareness about his usual thoughtlessness

jokes.text: It's a state-of-the-art food mash-'em-upper
jokes.setup: Homer revealing his thoughtful gift
jokes.punchline: Calling a food processor a 'mash-'em-upper' in his crude terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer vocabulary - reduces sophisticated appliance to crude descriptive terms

jokes.text: Your mom's gonna feel so good, it'll make mere love seem like chewing tinfoil
jokes.setup: Homer explaining how happy Marge will be
jokes.punchline: The bizarre comparison of love to chewing tinfoil
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist comparison - 'chewing tinfoil' is a perfect sensory opposite to feeling good

jokes.text: Finally, a supermarket with a clear premise: 'island something'
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on the new store
jokes.punchline: His reductive analysis of the tropical theme as just 'island something'
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer oversimplification of marketing concepts

jokes.text: It's like going to Hawaii without the murderous locals
jokes.setup: Homer comparing the store to Hawaii
jokes.punchline: The casual reference to 'murderous locals' in Hawaii
jokes.explanation: Dark humor twist on tropical vacation fantasy - unexpected and economical

jokes.text: Apu! What are you doing out of your natural habitat?
jokes.setup: Homer seeing Apu outside the Kwik-E-Mart
jokes.punchline: Referring to the convenience store as Apu's 'natural habitat' like he's an animal
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual dehumanization of Apu - fits character but somewhat dated humor

jokes.text: You keep your ice cream right beside the motor oil!
jokes.setup: Marge defending shopping at the new store
jokes.punchline: The absurd product placement at Kwik-E-Mart
jokes.explanation: Good observation about convenience store logic - functional but predictable

jokes.text: I have always considered you cash-giving cows to be my friends
jokes.setup: Apu defending his relationship with customers
jokes.punchline: Calling them 'cash-giving cows' while claiming friendship
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Apu's honesty about customer relationships while claiming affection

jokes.text: Friends are the only people you can betray!
jokes.setup: Apu saying friends don't betray friends
jokes.punchline: Homer's cynical logic about betrayal requiring trust
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark wisdom - perfectly economical and surprisingly insightful about human nature

jokes.text: Convenience forever, freshness never!
jokes.setup: Apu's passionate defense of his store
jokes.punchline: Battle cry that admits his food isn't fresh
jokes.explanation: Good rallying cry that undercuts itself - Apu's pride in questionable priorities

jokes.text: Oh, my God, this might be a Marge's birthday where I get sex!
jokes.setup: Homer excited about the celebrity call for Marge
jokes.punchline: His crude motivation and public declaration
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer oversharing - character-appropriate but predictable marital humor

jokes.text: Quick, before it suffocates!
jokes.setup: Lisa urging Marge to open a gift
jokes.punchline: Implying the gift is alive and in danger
jokes.explanation: Quick absurdist moment - economical but not deeply funny

jokes.text: The only golf we'll watch is ladies' golf, and we're not watching that
jokes.setup: Homer promising they won't watch golf
jokes.punchline: The qualifier that makes his promise meaningless
jokes.explanation: Standard sports sexism humor - economical but predictable

jokes.text: This yolk-separating camera-hog was supposed to call you and say happy birthday!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining what went wrong
jokes.punchline: His specific food-related insult for the celebrity chef
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific insult relating to cooking shows

jokes.text: A pity kiss?! That tears it!
jokes.setup: Marge giving Homer a consolatory kiss
jokes.punchline: Homer's outrage at receiving pity affection
jokes.explanation: Good Homer pride moment - his rejection of sympathy

jokes.text: Aw, he's trying to eat 'ewectwicity'
jokes.setup: The rabbit chewed through the phone cord
jokes.punchline: Lisa's baby-talk pronunciation of 'electricity'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay humor - cute but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Screw you, Marge Simpson! Don't read my books and don't make my recipes! May my curse follow you beyond the grave!
jokes.setup: Celebrity chef's mounting frustration
jokes.punchline: Escalating to supernatural curses over a missed phone call
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation to absurdity - supernatural curse over missed call is perfectly overblown

jokes.text: An audience full of church groups and Marines!
jokes.setup: Homer noting who's booing Marge on TV
jokes.punchline: The unlikely demographic combination at a cooking show
jokes.explanation: Funny demographic observation - good specificity in the unlikely audience

jokes.text: You can't strangle a boy on his mother's birthday. Juries hate that.
jokes.setup: Marge stopping Homer from strangling Bart
jokes.punchline: Her legal reasoning rather than moral objection
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - Marge's practical legal advice over moral concerns is perfectly twisted

jokes.text: No way, man. I'm a free-range kid.
jokes.setup: Homer ordering Bart into the rabbit cage
jokes.punchline: Bart applying poultry farming terms to himself
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting food production to childhood freedom - economical and smart

jokes.text: These cedar shavings are a hell of a lot better than my lumpy mattress
jokes.setup: Bart refusing to leave the rabbit cage
jokes.punchline: Revelation about the poor quality of his actual bed
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about family living conditions - explains Bart's preference

jokes.text: Awesome revenge, cutting your dad's face out of a piece of cardboard
jokes.setup: Milhouse seeing Bart's art project
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding the purpose as simple vandalism
jokes.explanation: Setup for the reveal - Milhouse's misunderstanding is functional but not hilarious

jokes.text: Every vandalism spree needs an obnoxious laugher
jokes.setup: Milhouse asking to join Bart's graffiti plan
jokes.punchline: His self-aware description of his role as annoying sidekick
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware character moment - Milhouse knowing exactly what he contributes

jokes.text: And these days, there's no newspapers so I have to put down Amazon Kindles
jokes.setup: Old man explaining his pigeon problem
jokes.punchline: Using expensive e-readers as newspaper replacement for bird droppings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant modern absurdity - the economics of using Kindles as bird cage liner is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: Don't just stare back at me, come up with a theory!
jokes.setup: Homer looking at his own graffitied image
jokes.punchline: Demanding his car help him figure out who the face belongs to
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness - talking to his car and missing the obvious

jokes.text: No, no. I'm too concerned with unemployment
jokes.setup: Homer asking if Bart knows about the graffiti
jokes.punchline: Bart's fake concern about adult economic issues
jokes.explanation: Good deflection - Bart claiming inappropriate adult concerns to avoid suspicion

jokes.text: I don't traffic in wordplay, Kent
jokes.setup: Kent using 'Vandal Van Gogh' pun
jokes.punchline: Wiggum rejecting wordplay while immediately using worse puns
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for Wiggum's hypocrisy - denying wordplay before diving into it

jokes.text: They're all artists, Lou. Why don't you open your eyes.
jokes.setup: Lou correcting Wiggum about Frank Gehry being an architect
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's dismissive response treating all creative fields as identical
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum moment - his intellectual condescension despite being wrong

jokes.text: you may have thumbed your nose at the police, and made yourself into a combination of Robin Hood, Luke Skywalker and, well, every rapper ever
jokes.setup: Wiggum addressing the graffiti artist
jokes.punchline: His increasingly broad cultural comparisons ending with 'every rapper ever'
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's confused cultural analysis - mixing fantasy heroes with musicians

jokes.text: Hey, after biting off a man's nose in a prison race war, selling pre-cooked Pad Thai to soccer moms is pretty darn sweet
jokes.setup: Snake talking about his new job
jokes.punchline: Comparing extreme prison violence to mundane food service
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark contrast - specific gruesome detail against suburban normalcy

jokes.text: Here, I'm not Jailbird, I'm just 'Bird.'
jokes.setup: Snake explaining his workplace transformation
jokes.punchline: Shortening his criminal nickname for civilian life
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail about rebranding for employment - economical and character-specific

jokes.text: Because I have a gun, you must stick them up now!
jokes.setup: Apu attempting to rob the store
jokes.punchline: His overly polite explanation of robbery mechanics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu moment - his excessive politeness even during armed robbery

jokes.text: For once, the Indian has been outsourced!
jokes.setup: Apu robbing his former workplace
jokes.punchline: Economic commentary on role reversal
jokes.explanation: Sharp economic observation with dark edge - plays on outsourcing stereotypes

jokes.text: Oh, sweet Vishnu the Destroyer, what have I done?
jokes.setup: Apu realizing he's robbing his replacement
jokes.punchline: Calling on a Hindu deity with destroyer epithet during moral crisis
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific religious exclamation with ironic deity choice

jokes.text: Wiggum just makes you do book reports for Ralph. But you have to make 'em so it looks like he's done it.
jokes.setup: Snake explaining the justice system
jokes.punchline: The difficulty of faking a child's homework level
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about having to dumb down your work to match a child's ability

jokes.text: Just throw in a couple of 'fur is murders.'
jokes.setup: Lisa bargaining for her silence about the graffiti
jokes.punchline: Wanting Homer to say PETA slogans as payment
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment - using blackmail for animal rights activism

jokes.text: Fine. Fur's murder. Everything's murder.
jokes.setup: Homer grudgingly agreeing to Lisa's demands
jokes.punchline: Expanding the concept to absurd extremes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Homer's nihilistic expansion of the murder concept

jokes.text: The jerk in the glasses did everything!
jokes.setup: Bart and Milhouse caught by street artists
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately throwing Milhouse under the bus
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart betrayal of Milhouse - character-appropriate but predictable

jokes.text: Here, drink this paint thinner.
jokes.setup: Milhouse having an asthma attack
jokes.punchline: Bart offering dangerous chemicals as medicine
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about friendship - Bart's casual endangerment of Milhouse

jokes.text: Is that graffiti or are you opening up a Mommy and Me?
jokes.setup: Street artist critiquing Bart's Reagan piece
jokes.punchline: Comparing political art to baby classes
jokes.explanation: Standard art world insult - comparing amateur work to suburban activities

jokes.text: Haw-haw!
jokes.setup: Nelson hearing Shepard Fairey's name
jokes.punchline: His signature laugh at the unusual name
jokes.explanation: Classic Nelson catchphrase - predictable but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Hey, I got an F in art. Mainly so it would say 'fart' on my report card.
jokes.setup: Artists praising Bart's talent
jokes.punchline: Bart's actual motivation for failing art class
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - sacrificing grades for juvenile humor

jokes.text: Bart, street art is not about questioning authority.
jokes.setup: Bart questioning the gallery concept
jokes.punchline: Artist contradicting the fundamental premise of rebellious art
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of art world commercialization - perfect contradiction of street art's supposed rebellion

jokes.text: At the opening, I could wear a sports jacket with a t-shirt and jeans!
jokes.setup: Milhouse excited about the gallery show
jokes.punchline: His specific enthusiasm for art world fashion clichés
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse moment - excitement about superficial art world signifiers

jokes.text: I poked your flab back in, square by square.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining his art process
jokes.punchline: The meticulous detail of depicting Homer's body
jokes.explanation: Good visual description of artistic process with body humor

jokes.text: Every successful corporation harbors a terrible secret. Theirs is that what they sell as chicken is actually monkey.
jokes.setup: Apu explaining why Swapper Jack's is closing
jokes.punchline: Escalating to bizarre food substitution conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Good conspiracy theory humor - specific and building to monkey reveal

jokes.text: Monkey, monkey, monkey!
jokes.setup: Homer asking about different chicken products
jokes.punchline: Apu's increasingly frustrated monkey responses
jokes.explanation: Functional escalation - repetition works for mounting frustration

jokes.text: Look at me, I'm Mr. Fatso! It's funny 'cause it's so preposterous.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy imitating the graffiti image
jokes.punchline: His self-aware commentary while being the actual model
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment - CBG analyzing humor while being the unwitting subject

jokes.text: Your son's made a fool of you in front of the whole town.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy realizing the truth about Mr. Fatso
jokes.punchline: Delivering the harsh truth to Homer
jokes.explanation: Functional revelation moment - moves plot forward with character insight

jokes.text: Go drive a car! Yes, master. Here, car! Here, boy!
jokes.setup: Homer commanding his car
jokes.punchline: Treating the car like a pet dog
jokes.explanation: Good Homer confusion about how machines work - treating car as pet

jokes.text: When a man isn't a hero to his son, he's nothing.
jokes.setup: Homer's reaction to being mocked publicly
jokes.punchline: His unexpectedly profound and sad realization
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's rare moment of genuine insight and vulnerability

jokes.text: I like how the painting makes more than I do.
jokes.setup: Visitor at the art gallery
jokes.punchline: Art market economics vs regular wages
jokes.explanation: Sharp economic observation about art world vs working class wages

jokes.text: $500! Whoo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Bart's vandalism making Homer's car worth more
jokes.punchline: Homer's excitement over modest financial gain
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer moment - small money making him instantly happy

jokes.text: What about the Hulk riding a rhinoceros? Would you stop him from laughing?
jokes.setup: Bart promising to defend Homer from mockery
jokes.punchline: Homer's increasingly specific hypothetical challengers
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation - Homer's weird mental scenarios

jokes.text: Hold it right there, Spray-candy Warhol!
jokes.setup: Wiggum arresting Bart
jokes.punchline: His art world nickname combining candy and Warhol
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum wordplay - combining art references with graffiti tools

jokes.text: Do you think people would be stupid enough to pay for something some amateur put on a wall for nothing?
jokes.setup: Wiggum explaining the sting operation
jokes.punchline: Accidentally describing the entire art market
jokes.explanation: Brilliant art world satire - Wiggum unknowingly critiques all contemporary art commerce

jokes.text: I spent 20 years putting up posters that said 'obey.'
jokes.setup: Fairey revealed as undercover cop
jokes.punchline: His art career as preparation for police work
jokes.explanation: Brilliant real-world art critique - Fairey's 'Obey' campaign as authoritarian training

jokes.text: I'm not in the business of helping out posers any more. Now, I just sell stuff to them.
jokes.setup: Fairey explaining his motivation
jokes.punchline: Complete commercialization of street art rebellion
jokes.explanation: Sharp critique of art world evolution from rebellion to commerce - cynical but accurate

tmdb_overview: When Bart decides to get back at Homer by becoming a graffiti artist, established street artists offer him a gallery show; a new health food store threatens to put Apu out of business.
